title: Carbon pricing in climate policy: seven reasons, complementary instruments, and political economy considerations
authors: Andrea Baranzini; Jeroen C. J. M. van den Bergh; Stefano Carattini; Richard B. Howarth; Emilio Padilla; Jordi Roca

tax_mentions.context: Reviews design, distributional impacts, and political acceptability of carbon taxes as a core climate policy instrument.
tax_mentions.page_or_section: Throughout; esp. Political Economy Issues, PE Issue 1
tax_mentions.notes: Synthesizes empirical literature on acceptance and design features, no original data.

tax_mentions.context: Describes Swiss 2015 referendum on replacing VAT with an energy tax and voters' opposition and preferences.
tax_mentions.page_or_section: PE Issue 1: Distributional Consequences of Carbon Pricing
tax_mentions.notes: Discusses vote outcome and determinants like inequality concerns, citing Carattini et al.

tax_mentions.context: Argues that recycling carbon tax revenues to households or green projects can improve fairness and acceptability.
tax_mentions.page_or_section: More than carbon pricing; PE Issue 1

excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: Cap-and-trade and EU-ETS are carbon markets allocating permits, not tax instruments on fuels or emissions.
excluded_policy_mentions.page_or_section: Seven arguments; PE Issue 6; Two ways of implementing carbon pricing

excluded_policy_mentions.exact_term_used_in_paper: subsidies for adopting renewable energy; innovation subsidies
excluded_policy_mentions.reason_it_is_not_a_carbon_or_fuel_tax: Innovation and adoption subsidies for renewables or efficiency are spending instruments, distinct from carbon or fuel taxation.
Fuel economy or technology standards regulate performance directly and do not impose a per-unit carbon or fuel tax.

inclusion_reason: Broad conceptual review of carbon pricing that explicitly discusses public and voter acceptability of carbon and energy taxes, emphasizing distributional equity, revenue use, and design features affecting support.
